It seems hard to believe that people with great abilities, as well as those who are ignorant, would make themselves look foolish and act like madmen, simply for the taste or excitement produced by strong liquors.
How powerful kings are to those who provoke them! How much more foolish then is it to provoke the King of kings!
To engage in quarrels is the greatest folly that can be. Yield, and even give up just demands, for peace’s sake.
He who labors and endures hardship in his seed-time for eternity will be properly diligent as to his earthly business.
Though many capable of giving wise counsel are silent, yet something may be drawn from them, which will reward those who obtain it.
It is hard to find people that have done, and will do more good than they speak, or care to hear spoken of.
A good man is not likely to experience uneasiness in deciding what he shall do, or in reflecting on what he has done, as those who walk in deceit. And his family fares better for his sake.
If great men are good men, they may do much good and prevent very much evil.
Some can say, Through grace, we are cleaner than we have been; but it was the work of the Holy Spirit.
See the various deceits people use, of which the love of money is the root. The Lord will not bless what is thus gotten.
Parents should observe their children that they may manage them accordingly.
All our powers and faculties are from God, and are to be employed for him.
Those that indulge themselves may expect to want necessaries, which should have been gotten by honest labor.
People use arts to get a good bargain and to buy cheap; whereas a person ought to be ashamed of fraud and a lie.

He who prefers true knowledge to riches follows the ways of religion and happiness. If we really believed this truth, the word of God would be valued as it deserves, and the world would lose its tempting influence.
Those ruin themselves who entangle themselves in rash suretyship. Also those who are in league with abandoned women. Place no confidence in either.
Wealth gotten by fraud may be sweet, for the carnal mind takes pleasure in the success of wicked devices; but it will be bitter in reflection.
Especially we need advice in spiritual warfare. The word and Spirit of God are the best counsellors in every point.
Those dearly buy their own praise who put confidence in a person because he speaks fairly.
An undutiful child will become very miserable. Never let him expect any peace or comfort.
An estate suddenly raised is often as suddenly ruined.
Wait on the Lord, attend his pleasure, and he will protect you.
A bargain made by fraud will prove a losing bargain in the end.
How can we form plans and conduct business independently of the Lord?
The evasions people often use with their own consciences show how false and deceitful man is.
Justice should crush the wicked, and separate them from the virtuous.
The rational soul and conscience are as a lamp within us, which should be used in examining our dispositions and motives with the revealed will of God.
Mercy and truth are the glories of God’s throne.
Both young and old have their advantages; and let neither despise or envy the other.

Severe rebukes sometimes do a great deal of good. But such is the corruption of nature, that people are reluctant to be rebuked for their sins. If God uses severe afflictions to purify our hearts and fit us for his service, we have cause to be very thankful.
